name: Tok Pisin
notes: Tok Pisin — English-lexifier creole of Papua New Guinea, one of its official languages. The orthography is phonemic: 21 Latin letters, five vowels /i e a o u/, digraphs ⟨ai au oi⟩ for the diphthongs and ⟨ng⟩ for /ŋ/ (also /ŋɡ/ in some words). ⟨r⟩ is usually an alveolar tap [ɾ]. Final obstruents are devoiced (pik 'pig'). More educated speakers may distinguish more vowels under English influence.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Tok Pisin.

ancestors.notes: English lexifier; Tok Pisin of Papua New Guinea. Holm (1989:527-539); APiCS ch. 22 (Smith & Siegel).

Tolai (Kuanua) and other Oceanic (Malayo-Polynesian) substrate: 5-vowel system, predicate marker i, transitive suffix -im reflecting Oceanic *-i. Tolai itself is unregistered; nearest registered node is Malayo-Polynesian. Mosel (1980).
